description:
Goal: close the prequel arc cleanly and hand back a smaller remaining problem. Signal: docs, tests, and ticket text all show that the remaining work in `fz-rh2.8.2.3.3` is just the outer JIT/AOT consumer switch and cleanup, if any remains.

Change:
- update docs and telemetry/test expectations to match the new native codegen boundary
- remove temporary notes/scaffolding used while splitting the codegen seam
- rewrite `fz-rh2.8.2.3.3` to describe only the remaining consumer move

Why:
- this arc only pays off if it leaves the queue cleaner and the next ticket smaller than the one we paused

Checks:
- no temporary contract scaffolding is left behind without a ticket
- `fz-rh2.8.2.3.3` is either obviously tractable or obviously obsolete by the end
title: Compiler2 native backends: close the seam split and shrink the remaining switch
